note: Real source of SecUSo Privacy Friendly Sudoku, pinned just before commit 85895f3 ('[fix] changes foreground service type to special use') so that commit can be edit 01 -- a genuine self-contained real changeset (one line in the vendored file). Not the whole app (Java-primary + a Kotlin backup shim, game/board UI, backup-api) -- an acyclic real SUBGRAPH covering the distinctive behaviors this app was picked for: the real foreground puzzle-generator Service (controller/GeneratorService.java, foregroundServiceType specialUse), the real qqwing sudoku-generation algorithm (controller/qqwing/{QQWing,LogItem,PrintStyle,LogType,Action,Symmetry}.java, pure logic, ~1900 lines, no Android deps beyond android.util.Log), and the legacy-SQLite persistence layer it saves generated puzzles through (controller/database/{DatabaseHelper,columns/*,migration/*,model/*}.java, controller/Symbol.java, game/{GameDifficulty,GameType}.java). Same acyclic-subgraph technique as pedometer/todo-list/notes: pin real leaf files, author minimal scaffolding only where the real files reference UI/backup they don't otherwise need -- ui/MainActivity.java is a stand-in launcher (the real GeneratorService only builds an Intent targeting it for the notification's PendingIntent) and PFSudoku.kt is a stand-in Application subclass providing just the CHANNEL_ID constant GeneratorService reads (the real PFSudoku wires the :backup-api submodule, excluded per the standard rationale). controller/NewLevelManager.java is ALSO a stand-in (real file is 525 lines of game-flow orchestration; GeneratorService only reads its PRE_SAVES_MIN constant via a static import). Needs no extra androidx beyond androidx.core (JobIntentService/NotificationCompat/ServiceCompat) and androidx.annotation (StringRes) on the compile classpath -- everything else here is plain android.* / java.*. Real strings.xml and the mdpi launcher icon are vendored as-is (only mdpi density, same as the other SecUSo entries); game/GameType.java's real icon_default_* drawables are vendored too since its enum constructor references them directly.
